List Comprehension
code:
# Generate squares of even numbers using list comprehension
numbers = range(1, 21)

squares_of_evens = [n**2 for n in numbers if n % 2 == 0]

print("Squares of even numbers:")
print(squares_of_evens)

# Nested comprehension - matrix flatten
matrix = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]]
flattened = [num for row in matrix for num in row]
print("Flattened matrix:", flattened)

title: Dictionary Frequency
NumPy Matplotlib Data Viz
code:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Generate sample data
x = np.linspace(0, 10, 100)
y1 = np.sin(x)
y2 = np.cos(x)

# Create the plot
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.plot(x, y1, label='sin(x)', color='cyan', linewidth=2)
plt.plot(x, y2, label='cos(x)', color='magenta', linewidth=2)
plt.title('Sine and Cosine Waves')
plt.xlabel('x')
plt.ylabel('y')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.show()

print("Mean of sin(x):", np.mean(y1))
print("Std Dev of cos(x):", np.std(y2))
